metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC4's proving command by naming the specific anchor strings (or regex patterns) that `test_session_shims.py` must assert are present in `docs/session-id-shims.md` — one per required doc element (e.g. `hooks.SessionStart`, the G1 instability warning text, and the not-in-profile warning text).
findings.finding: AC4's proving command ('a doc-content assert in test_session_shims.py') does not specify what strings or patterns are asserted, leaving the executor to decide — a trivially-passing grep on any substring satisfies the letter of the AC without verifying the three required doc elements (settings.json snippet, G1 id-instability caveat, not-in-profile guidance).
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'a doc-content assert in test_session_shims.py' with: 'Proof: test_session_shims.py reads docs/session-id-shims.md and asserts all three of: (1) the substring `"hooks"` + `"SessionStart"` (install snippet), (2) a phrase matching `session_id.*not.*stable` or the literal string documenting G1, and (3) the phrase `do not export REBAR_SESSION_ID` (or equivalent) warning against shell-profile use.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the script (and AC2 assertion) to emit `export AI_AGENT=claude-code_<ver>` by reading the version from the SessionStart JSON payload's `version` field (or a CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION env var if available), falling back to bare `claude-code` only when version is absent — matching the `claude-code_<ver>` convention established in docs/config.md, docs/event-schema.md, and session_id.py.
findings.finding: The script writes `export AI_AGENT=claude-code` (bare, no version), but every existing codebase artifact that establishes the AI_AGENT convention — docs/config.md, docs/event-schema.md, src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py, and tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py — consistently shows the expected format as `claude-code_<ver>` (e.g. `claude-code_1.2.3`). Emitting a bare tag causes the harness-provenance record for all local-CLI users to omit version information and diverge from the documented pattern, reducing the forensic value of claim_harness.
findings.location: What section — Hook script paragraph: 'exports ... export AI_AGENT=claude-code'
findings.suggested_fix: In the hook script, after extracting `session_id`, also extract the version (e.g. `version=$(echo "$json" | python3 -c 'import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get("version",""))')`) and compose `AI_AGENT=claude-code${version:+_$version}`. In AC2, assert `export AI_AGENT=claude-code_<ver>` with a mock stdin that includes a `version` field, and assert bare `claude-code` when version is absent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the `AI_AGENT` value the script writes (`claude-code` bare) against the established `claude-code_<ver>` convention documented in `docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, and `session_id.py` — either update the plan to emit `claude-code` and document that bare-tag is intentional for local-CLI (no version available at hook time), or specify that the script should emit `claude-code_<claude_version>` using the Claude Code version from the hook payload if available.
findings.finding: The plan writes `AI_AGENT=claude-code` (bare tag, no version suffix), but the established convention throughout the codebase — `docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, `src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py`, and `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` — consistently uses `claude-code_<ver>` (version-suffixed). This is an unverified capability assertion about the correct `AI_AGENT` value for the Claude Code harness.
findings.location: What section — Hook script description: 'export AI_AGENT=claude-code'; AC2: 'writes … `export AI_AGENT=claude-code` to that file'
findings.suggested_fix: If version is available in the SessionStart JSON payload (e.g. a `version` field), emit `AI_AGENT=claude-code_<version>`; if not available, document explicitly in the plan that `claude-code` (bare, no version) is the intentional local-CLI value and update the convention docs to reflect bare-tag as a valid variant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the exact `AI_AGENT` value the shim should write: either specify version-extraction (e.g., from the SessionStart JSON envelope's `claude_version` field, or from `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` env var if available) to match the documented `claude-code_<ver>` convention, or explicitly state that bare `claude-code` is a deliberate fallback when no version is available and update AC2 to reflect the accepted vocabulary.
findings.finding: The plan writes 'export AI_AGENT=claude-code' (bare, unversioned), but the established AI_AGENT vocabulary in the codebase is 'claude-code_<ver>' (version-suffixed). The downstream consumers of claim_harness (ticket_state.schema.json, the LLM schema 'chn' field, and test_harness_provenance.py) treat the value as an opaque string, but the documented enum-like vocabulary uniformly includes a version suffix. The plan never specifies how to obtain the Claude Code version, nor whether bare 'claude-code' is an intentional vocabulary deviation, leaving an executor to either silently diverge from the schema convention or invent their own version-extraction mechanism.
findings.location: What section — Hook script spec; AC2
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section and AC2, specify: if the SessionStart JSON contains a version field (e.g. `version` or `claude_version`), write `AI_AGENT=claude-code_<ver>`; otherwise fall back to the bare `claude-code`. Update AC2's assertion to check for this pattern rather than the exact static string.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test case (e.g. `claude_code_noop_malformed_json`) that feeds malformed JSON and/or simulates python3 failure, asserting exit 0 and no write to the env file, to cover the 'never blocks the session' guarantee end-to-end.
findings.finding: AC3 tests only blank/missing `session_id` and unset `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE`, but does not cover malformed/unparseable JSON on stdin or a `python3` execution failure — both of which can occur in the wild yet are omitted despite the plan explicitly claiming 'robust' python3 parsing and a hard requirement that the hook 'never blocks the session' (exit 0).
findings.suggested_fix: In `test_session_shims.py`, add a parametrized noop case: pipe `'{"bad":true'` (truncated JSON) as stdin and assert the script exits 0 and the env file is empty/unmodified. Also add a case that sets `PATH` to exclude python3 and asserts the same. Update AC3 text to name these cases explicitly.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default behavior for malformed JSON input (e.g. 'treat as noop, exit 0, suppress stderr') and add a corresponding AC or extend AC3 to cover this case.
findings.finding: The plan states python3 is used for 'robust' JSON parsing but never specifies the exit behavior for malformed/unparseable JSON input — an executor must choose between exit 0 (noop, consistent with 'never blocks the session') and exit non-zero (which would block the session).
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Malformed JSON on stdin (e.g. `echo "{bad" | ...`) is also a no-op with exit 0.' and document in the What section: 'Any JSON parse error is silently swallowed; the script always exits 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the 'same value on resume' idempotency claim with the G1 'id not stable across --resume' caveat — either reword the script description to say 'last export wins (id may differ on resume)' or clarify that 'clear/compact' is stable while '--resume' is not.
findings.finding: The script behavior description claims re-firing on resume/compact is idempotent ('re-exports the same value'), but the G1 caveat in the docs description explicitly states the id is NOT stable across --resume/--continue/fork. These directly contradict each other: if the id changes on resume, the hook re-fires with a NEW id, not the 'same value'.
findings.location: What section — Hook script paragraph ('Idempotent: re-firing on resume/clear/compact simply re-exports the same value') vs. Docs paragraph ('the id is NOT stable across --resume/--continue/fork/subagents (epic gotcha G1)')
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Hook script paragraph to: 'Idempotent within a single uninterrupted session (re-exporting the same value on clear/compact); note that --resume/--continue/fork start a new SessionStart with a different id, consistent with G1.' This aligns with the G1 caveat without removing the valid fire-and-forget property.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a clause to AC4 (or a new AC) asserting that docs/session-id-shims.md contains a cross-link to docs/config.md, matching the deliverable described in the What section.
findings.finding: The What section describes a deliverable — 'Cross-links docs/config.md' — inside the Docs description, but no AC verifies that this cross-link exists. It is an uncovered deliverable.
findings.location: What section — Docs paragraph ('Cross-links docs/config.md') vs. Acceptance Criteria section (AC4 only asserts settings.json snippet, G1 caveat, and session-scoped guidance)
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC4: '…and a cross-reference link to docs/config.md. Proof: grep or doc-content assert in test_session_shims.py confirms the link is present.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that write errors and python3 invocation failures emit a diagnostic to stderr (even a single line) before exiting 0, so users can detect misconfiguration without blocking the session.
findings.finding: Write failures to CLAUDE_ENV_FILE (e.g., permission denied, disk full) and python3 invocation failures are silently swallowed — the script always exits 0 with no stderr output or log entry — leaving the user with no signal that REBAR_SESSION_ID was never populated.
findings.location: What section — hook script description; AC line 3 ('never blocks the session (fire-and-forget)'); AC line 3 ('silent no-op')
findings.suggested_fix: In the script spec, add: 'On write failure or python3 error, emit a single warning line to stderr (e.g., `echo "[rebar] claude-code-session-start: failed to write CLAUDE_ENV_FILE" >&2`) before exiting 0; document this in the install guide so users know to check stderr.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge the concurrent-append race in the script spec (or docs) and specify a mitigation — e.g., use a file lock (`flock`), write to a temp file and `mv`, or document that only the outermost session should set CLAUDE_ENV_FILE.
findings.finding: Concurrent appends to CLAUDE_ENV_FILE from parallel hook invocations (subagents, forked sessions) are not addressed; simultaneous `>>` appends from multiple processes can interleave, producing a corrupt or partial export line.
findings.location: What section — hook script description ('APPENDS ... to it'); gotcha G1 mention of subagents
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the script: `( flock -x 9; echo 'export REBAR_SESSION_ID=...' >> "$CLAUDE_ENV_FILE"; echo 'export AI_AGENT=claude-code' >> "$CLAUDE_ENV_FILE" ) 9>>"$CLAUDE_ENV_FILE".lock` — or document that CLAUDE_ENV_FILE should not be set in subagent contexts to avoid the race.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one end-to-end or dogfooding AC that verifies the user-observable outcome — e.g., 'After following the install guide and running a local session, `REBAR_SESSION_ID` is present in the environment during that session (verified by a test that sources the env file and asserts the var is set).'
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria validate only internal script correctness (correct file output, exit 0) but do not include a user-observable validation that session provenance is actually end-to-end functional after install — no before/after workflow note, no dogfooding step, and no operational signal (e.g., 'after running the hook, REBAR_SESSION_ID appears in a claim event').
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'After sourcing `$CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` (as Claude Code does), `REBAR_SESSION_ID` and `AI_AGENT` are present in the environment. Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_session_shims.py -k claude_code_end_to_end -q` sources the env file written by the script and asserts both vars are exported.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm with ticket 7656 that `docs/session-id-shims.md` is the shared target page, or explicitly record the agreed docs file name in both this ticket and 7656 so the coordination is enforceable.
findings.finding: The plan's OUT-scope exclusion states '(S6 / 7656, which extend the same docs page)' — asserting that ticket 7656 will extend `docs/session-id-shims.md`. But ticket 7656's description says only 'Per-harness install docs' without naming that file, so 7656 may create a separate page or extend docs/config.md instead. A false coordination claim here invisibly deletes the requirement that 7656 extend the same page.
findings.location: Scope section — OUT line: 'Codex/Cursor shims (S6 / 7656, which extend the same docs page)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a concrete cross-reference in both tickets: in this ticket's docs deliverable, add a comment block listing the filename explicitly; amend 7656's file_impact to name `docs/session-id-shims.md` (not just `docs/`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `docs/config.md` as an edit target: add a back-reference from its harness-shim prose (§session-provenance) to the new `docs/session-id-shims.md` install guide.
findings.finding: docs/config.md already documents the Claude Code SessionStart shim mechanism (line 345: 'its SessionStart shim exporting REBAR_SESSION_ID') and the AI_AGENT per-harness shims (line 352), but the plan does not enumerate docs/config.md as a consumer requiring a back-reference update to the new docs/session-id-shims.md install guide.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section under Docs, add: 'Also updates docs/config.md to add a cross-reference from the existing per-harness-shim prose (the "Harness provenance" paragraph) to docs/session-id-shims.md.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a reference (URL or version-pinned quote) from Claude Code's official hook documentation confirming: (a) `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the correct env-injection variable name, (b) the append-with-`>>` semantic is supported, and (c) the `SessionStart` stdin payload uses the key `session_id` — inline in the plan's What section or as a comment in the script.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` (append-with-`>>`) is 'the documented Claude Code env-injection mechanism' but provides no citation, version reference, or link to Claude Code's own documentation confirming this variable name, the append semantics, and that the file is re-sourced for the running session. If the actual mechanism differs (different var name, single-write not append, file must pre-exist, or read only at startup), the shim silently no-ops for all local-CLI users — the stated primary use case.
findings.location: What section — Hook script description: 'when `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is set, APPENDS … to it (the documented Claude Code env-injection mechanism — append with `>>`)'; also AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the correct env var name for Claude Code's env-injection mechanism by citing the Claude Code documentation URL or `--help` output, and add the reference to `docs/session-id-shims.md`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is 'the documented Claude Code env-injection mechanism' but this specific env var name cannot be verified anywhere in the repo. If the actual Claude Code CLI uses a different variable (e.g., `CLAUDE_ENVIRONMENT_FILE` or no such mechanism at the targeted version), the script silently no-ops for all users, defeating the feature entirely.
findings.location: What section — Hook script description: 'when `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is set, APPENDS ... to it (the documented Claude Code env-injection mechanism)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a footnote/link in the plan and in `docs/session-id-shims.md` citing the exact Claude Code docs page (e.g., https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/hooks) that confirms `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` and its append semantics. If that page is unavailable, add a spike AC: run `claude --help` / inspect a real SessionStart hook invocation to confirm the var name before merging.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the exact Claude Code `SessionStart` hook event name and the JSON field name for the session id (e.g., by citing Claude Code hook documentation or a real hook invocation trace), and update the AC2 stdin fixture and docs accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the Claude Code `SessionStart` hook delivers a JSON envelope with a `session_id` field on stdin, but this specific field name is unverified. If the actual field is `sessionId`, `id`, or `session.id`, the python3 JSON parse succeeds but extracts an empty value — another silent no-op.
findings.location: What section — Hook script description: 'Reads the SessionStart JSON envelope from stdin (field `session_id`)'; AC2: 'Given stdin `{"session_id":"abc123"}`'
findings.suggested_fix: Reference the Claude Code hooks documentation (or run a probe session with `cat` as the hook to capture real stdin JSON) and record the confirmed field name as a comment in the script and as a pinned reference in `docs/session-id-shims.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the initial section skeleton of `docs/session-id-shims.md` (e.g., a 'Claude Code' section now, with stub headings for 'Codex' and 'Cursor' marked as TODO / coming in S6/7656) so that story 7656 can extend rather than restructure the page.
findings.finding: The plan states that ticket 7656 (Codex/Cursor shims) will 'extend the same docs page' (docs/session-id-shims.md), but does not define the page structure (e.g., section stubs for Codex and Cursor) that this story must lay down to allow 7656 to extend without stomping — making 7656 an unenumerated structural consumer of the doc artifact.
findings.location: Scope section: 'OUT: Codex/Cursor shims (S6 / 7656, which extend the same docs page)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
